title: Drafting Services

alternatives.name: AEC construction-document and detailing service firms
inclusion_criteria: Firm is classified in NAICS 541340 and a plurality of drafting-service revenue comes from architectural, structural-steel, civil-site, as-built, or drawing-checking deliverables for building and construction projects.
Core work converts specifications, sketches, surveys, scans, or design intent into CAD, BIM, shop-drawing, construction-document, or record-drawing deliverables.
The operator sells drafting, modeling, detailing, checking, or document-production capacity and is not principally the licensed architect or engineer of record.
exclusion_criteria: Mechanical-machine, industrial-equipment, process-piping, electrical, or electronic-system drafting supplies the plurality of drafting revenue.
No single technical domain supplies a plurality because the firm principally sells cross-disciplinary drafting consulting, conversion, overflow staffing, or other general drafting capacity.
The establishment is principally an architectural, engineering, surveying, interior-design, industrial-design, software, construction, or fabrication business rather than a drafting-services firm.
alternatives.rationale: The reproducible base allocation applies 55.5% of 2022 Economic Census NAICS 541340 revenue to the normalized nine-firm target-band estimate: architectural-project drafting 34.0%, steel detailing 14.5%, civil-site drafting 1.1%, as-built drawings 4.5%, and drawing checking/revision 1.4%. This yields 4.995 firm-equivalents, rounded to 5.00. Business sources confirm that CAD/BIM documentation, steel/shop detailing, scan-to-CAD, and as-built production coexist in this operating family. The range is wide because product lines coexist within establishments and industrywide revenue weights do not directly classify the nine target-band firms.

alternatives.name: Cross-disciplinary drafting consulting and general-service firms
inclusion_criteria: Firm is classified in NAICS 541340 and principally sells drafting consulting, overflow production, file conversion, model or document coordination, or other drafting capacity across multiple technical domains.
No AEC or industrial drafting domain supplies a supported plurality of drafting-service revenue.
exclusion_criteria: AEC construction documents, structural detailing, civil-site drawings, or as-built work supplies the plurality of drafting revenue.
Mechanical, process-piping, electrical, electronic, machine, or industrial-equipment drafting supplies the plurality of drafting revenue.
The operator principally supplies professional architecture, engineering, surveying, design, software, fabrication, or construction rather than drafting services.
alternatives.rationale: The base allocation applies the combined 20.7% revenue share for drafting consulting services (12.3%) and other drafting services (8.4%) to nine target-band firms, yielding 1.863 and rounded to 1.86. Operators advertising architectural, mechanical, electrical, conversion, and as-built capabilities demonstrate this cross-disciplinary model. Its supported range overlaps the selected archetype because consulting and other-drafting product lines may represent AEC work in target-band firms.

alternatives.name: Industrial, mechanical, process, and electrical drafting firms
inclusion_criteria: Firm is classified in NAICS 541340 and a plurality of drafting-service revenue comes from machines, mechanical systems, industrial-equipment layouts, process piping, flow diagrams, or electrical and electronic systems.
Deliverables principally support manufacturing, plant, equipment, product, process, or electrical-system documentation rather than building architecture or structural-steel detailing.
exclusion_criteria: Architectural, structural-steel, civil-site, building as-built, or other AEC documentation supplies the plurality of drafting revenue.
The firm principally provides engineering design, equipment manufacturing, fabrication, installation, or software rather than standalone drafting services.
Cross-disciplinary consulting or general drafting is the principal service and no industrial domain supplies a supported plurality.
alternatives.rationale: The base allocation applies the combined 12.1% industry revenue share for mechanical drafting (9.7%), process-piping drafting (1.1%), and electrical/electronic drafting (1.3%) to nine target-band firms, yielding 1.089 and rounded to 1.09. The range allows for concentration of higher-value industrial work among larger firms and for overlap with general drafting consulting.

residual.name: Adjacent design, document transformation, display, and unclassified services
residual.rationale: This is the remaining 11.7% of the nine-firm base after allocating 55.5% to AEC documentation and detailing, 20.7% to cross-disciplinary consulting and other drafting, and 12.1% to industrial-systems drafting. It retains interior or industrial design, display and presentation, document transformation, data capture, copying, fabrication, construction, professional design, suppressed drafting products, and firms with no classifiable plurality.

sources.title: 2022 Economic Census EC2200NAPCSINDPRD: Industry by Products for the U.S. and States
U.S. Census Bureau Statistics of U.S. Businesses
AEC BIM Services CAD and BIM drafting consulting operating model
The Drafting Company CAD conversion, BIM, and scan-to-CAD workflow
OctaveCAD cross-disciplinary electrical, mechanical, and architectural drafting services
Collective AEC architectural CAD and structural-steel detailing service structure

target_archetype.selection_basis: Start with the normalized v4.2 estimate of nine firms in the $1–10M EBITDA band. Allocate base firm-equivalents mechanically from mutually exclusive groupings of 2022 Economic Census product-revenue shares: 55.5% AEC documentation and detailing = 5.00; 20.7% cross-disciplinary drafting consulting and other drafting = 1.86; 12.1% industrial-systems drafting = 1.09; remaining 11.7% = 1.05 residual. AEC documentation and detailing has the largest base count and is selected. This is an outcome-blind proxy allocation rather than observed classification of the nine firms. The two largest bases are not within 10%, but their supported count ranges of 2.7–6.8 and 1.0–3.6 overlap, so selection_uncertainty is true and the primary record remains singular.

value_basis.scope: Employee and controllable-contractor cash costs used to deliver the selected AEC construction-document and detailing archetype's controlled value added, with class-neutral functional weights broad enough to remain applicable under plausible reclassification into cross-disciplinary or industrial drafting.
value_basis.controllable_value_added_boundary: R_cva equals recognized revenue of the selected AEC documentation and detailing operator less only contemporaneously documented consideration contractually or economically owed to an outside provider and not controlled as value added by the operator. Drafting, modeling, detailing, checking, project coordination, document control, and integrated subcontract production directed by the operator remain inside R_cva.
approved_pass_through_categories: Client-reimbursed permit or filing fees paid to a government authority
Client-reimbursed printing, plotting, copying, scanning, shipping, or delivery purchased from an outside provider
Outside licensed architect, engineer, surveyor, testing, or specialist professional service separately identified for the client and not directed or integrated as the operator's drafting value added
Client-specific third-party data, imagery, or document-acquisition charge separately identified and economically owed to the outside provider

roles.title: Hands-on CAD, BIM, detailing, and drawing production
roles.rationale: Class-neutral bounded judgment for employee and controllable-contractor cash cost spent producing models, drawings, details, and revisions; it does not state a removable fraction.

roles.title: Technical checking, standards compliance, and quality assurance
roles.rationale: Class-neutral bounded judgment for review work separate from initial drafting and model production; it does not state a removable fraction.

roles.title: Project management, scheduling, and client or design-team coordination
roles.rationale: Class-neutral bounded judgment for project and client coordination not included in production or technical checking; it does not state a removable fraction.

roles.title: Document control, file conversion, data administration, and CAD/BIM technical support
roles.rationale: Class-neutral bounded judgment for document, file, and production-system support distinct from drafting output; it does not state a removable fraction.

roles.title: Sales, operating management, finance, and general administration
roles.rationale: Class-neutral bounded judgment for remaining commercial leadership and administrative cash cost; it does not state a removable fraction.

value_basis.rationale: No source directly measures the combined employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost role mix for target-band AEC drafting and detailing firms. The normalized dataset's 4-digit 5413 occupation mix spans architecture, engineering, inspection, surveying, and related businesses and has no contractor-cost bridge, so it is not used as target evidence. The five source-free ESTIMATE/NONE functional weights are a class-neutral assumption, sum exactly to one, and freeze only the cost basis—not any removable fraction, implementation outcome, or economic result.
caveats: Do not substitute the normalized 4-digit 5413 occupation shares for a target-specific cash-cost basis.
A subcontracted drafter, modeler, or detailer whose work is directed and integrated by the operator is controllable contractor cash cost and must not also be deducted from R_cva as pass-through.
An approved category is deductible only when contemporaneous evidence supports its base and endpoints; category approval is not a numeric assumption or evidence claim.
Software, ordinary occupancy, equipment, and general overhead are not pass-through merely because a third party supplies them.
These weights allocate employee plus controllable-contractor cash cost only; they do not assert that any role cost is removable or avoidable.
The assumption basis has NONE evidence quality and keeps downstream readiness UNPROVEN; unsupported monetary primitives remain MISSING rather than being inferred from the weights.

source_hints.source: Target-firm general ledger, revenue-recognition policy, project invoices, contracts, vendor bills, and job-cost records
source_hints.why: Needed to establish recognized revenue and separately documented third-party pass-through categories without deducting controlled subcontract drafting twice.

source_hints.source: Target-firm payroll, benefits cash records, contractor ledgers, 1099 detail, time records, and job-cost data mapped to the frozen functional roles
source_hints.why: Needed to test or replace the class-neutral assumption with target-specific employee and controllable-contractor cash costs and defensible role weights.

source_hints.source: Direct workflow observation, drawing revision logs, quality records, production-system records, time studies, and implementation vendor documents
source_hints.why: Needed to bound operational realization and investment by frozen role without treating task-time change as removable cash cost.

source_hints.source: Target-firm contracts, rate cards, bid histories, renewal cohorts, utilization records, and client interviews
source_hints.why: Needed to support commercial retention under fixed-fee, hourly, unit-priced, overflow-capacity, and pass-through billing arrangements.

source_hints.source: Closed drafting-services transactions, broker records, ownership registries, outreach logs, and bid or exclusivity records for a defined reference buyer
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish total firms from sale-available firms and from targets the reference buyer can access and win.

source_hints.source: Closed target-company deal documents, quality-of-earnings reports, and lender materials with paid and downside-exit multiples
source_hints.why: Needed to support entry and state-consistent downside-exit valuation for the selected archetype.

source_hints.source: Longitudinal business-register, tax-status, or credit-file cohorts for independent drafting-service firms of comparable size and business model
source_hints.why: Needed to support constant-price year-five operator-controlled value-added demand and distinguish failure from merger, rename, or owner exit.

controllable_value_added: What recognized-revenue base and range are supported for target-band AEC documentation and detailing firms, on what accounting basis and period?

Which approved outside-professional, printing, scanning, filing, data-acquisition, or delivery costs are contemporaneously documented third-party pass-through rather than operator-controlled value added?
What target-specific employee cash cost, controllable contractor cash cost, and role weights can be supported with MED/HIGH observed or calculated evidence?
How is controllable subcontract drafting kept in the V cash-cost numerator and excluded from pass-through deductions so no cost is counted twice?
If positive bounded R_cva or employee and controllable-contractor cash-cost amounts cannot be established, which monetary primitives remain MISSING?
operational_realization: For each evidenced target-specific role, which workflows are affected, what cash-cost fraction is genuinely removable or avoidable through evidenced growth, and what direct evidence supports each endpoint?
What five-year realization schedule, implementation investment, review burden, error or rework risk, interoperability constraint, and ongoing operating cost apply?

How do supported implementation ranges change across architectural documentation, steel detailing, as-built, cross-disciplinary, and industrial drafting workflows without changing the selected record?
commercial_retention: What pricing basis, contract term, utilization pattern, capacity commitment, quality obligation, and client response determine the discounted five-year retention schedule?
How do fixed-fee, hourly, per-sheet, per-model, dedicated-team, and reimbursable arrangements affect retention without changing V?
sale_availability: How many selected-archetype firms in the EBITDA band are sale-available during five years, and what observed or safely calculated evidence supports the base and endpoints?
How are architecture, engineering, fabrication, software, staffing, inactive, captive, and already-consolidated firms excluded from the sale-available denominator?
buyer_access_win: For a stated reference buyer, what share of sale-available targets is geographically, commercially, and operationally accessible?
What supported win rate applies after seller choice, competition, diligence, and reference-buyer constraints, without duplicating sale availability?
valuation_robustness: What closed-deal evidence supports paid entry and state-consistent downside-exit multiples for target-band AEC drafting and detailing firms?
How do customer concentration, owner production, project backlog, contractor dependence, professional-liability exposure, and mixed engineering or design revenue affect supported endpoints?
operator_survival: What evidence supports year-five constant-price demand for operator-controlled AEC drafting and detailing value added after removing documented pass-through?
How are mergers, trade-name changes, conversion into engineering or staffing models, and owner exit distinguished from operating failure?

special_notes: Archetype selection and the class-neutral controllable-value-added boundary were frozen before any research into economic outputs, implementation outcomes, commercial capture, buyability, valuation, or verdicts.
The normalized target-band estimate is nine firms and has LOW quality; product-revenue allocation yields reproducible base counts but does not observe the business model of those nine firms.
Base archetype counts sum to 7.95 and cover 88.3% of the nine-firm estimate; the 1.05 residual completes the base total to 9.00.
The top-two supported archetype count ranges overlap, so selection_uncertainty is true and downstream readiness must remain UNPROVEN.
No numeric recognized-revenue or pass-through assumption is frozen in this specification: the runner must source a positive bounded R_cva or leave the denominator and V MISSING.
Documented pass-through is deducted from recognized revenue only once; controlled subcontract drafting stays in controllable contractor cash cost and cannot also reduce R_cva.
The V value basis is a source-free ESTIMATE/NONE class-neutral ASSUMPTION with five role weights summing to one; it supplies no removability or outcome claim and keeps readiness UNPROVEN.
The primary record describes only AEC construction-document and detailing service firms, not all of NAICS 541340.
